project_description: Practical and theoretical sessions on AI. Hosted by Erick.
goal: Increase team knowledge of AI and establish a baseline
title: Launch AI Training sessions
details: Kick off the recurring AI training program. Define session format; speaker cadence; and shared documentation space. Establish the training backlog that will carry the team through the year.

project_description: A common approach to manage projects using JIRA
goal: Full monitoring and management of designers' and researchers' activity.
title: Introducing JIRA process for researchers
details: Roll out the new JIRA workflow for UX researchers. Define ticket types; field requirements; and tagging conventions. Run a guided onboarding session and collect initial friction points. Baseline process before the field-reduction work.

project_description: A common approach to manage projects using JIRA
title: Reducing 30% of the fields in JIRA
details: Audit all JIRA field configurations used by the design team. Remove redundant or unused fields and simplify the ticket template to reduce cognitive load. Document the new streamlined schema. Prerequisite for achieving full engagement in S192.

project_description: A common approach to manage projects using JIRA
title: 50% JIRA Engagement for Researchers
details: Audit all JIRA field configurations used by the design team. Remove redundant or unused fields and simplify the ticket template to reduce cognitive load. Document the new streamlined schema. Prerequisite for achieving full engagement in S192.

project_description: An AI-powered tool that reviews design output and scores alignment with the Design System standards
goal: x2 Governance and Consistency in NICE apps; using AI support.
title: Kick off Audit AI Assistant for Design System
details: Kick off the AI governance assistant project. Define scope; assign responsibilities; and prototype the first use case (component usage scanning or token drift detection).

project_description: Practical and theoretical sessions on AI. Hosted by Erick.
title: When to use Opus: how to get the most out of research
details: Practical session on choosing the right Claude model for UX research. Demo live synthesis of user interviews; compare Opus vs Sonnet; and share a reusable prompt library for research workflows. Follows the program launch; sets the foundation for the tool sessions ahead.

project_description: An AI assistant that streamlines JIRA usage for designers and managers; reducing manual effort and improving tracking accuracy
goal: Reduce 50% of time in JIRA for designers and managers; to manage/create tasks and for create reports.
title: POC: JIRA AI Assistant for managers and employees
details: Proof of concept for the AI Assistant integration in JIRA for managers and employees. Automate ticket summarisation; priority suggestions; and sprint planning inputs. First measurable step toward time reduction; targets 30% saving.

project_description: Activities related to sharing work across the team.
title: Sprint DEMO; will include all sites once in release
details: Expanding the Sprint DEMO to also include the USA site.

project_description: Practical and theoretical sessions on AI. Hosted by Erick.
title: Claude in GitHub and development
details: Hands-on session on Claude Code integration via GitHub. Cover PR review automation; code generation for design tokens and specs; and how designers can contribute to codebases using Claude as co-pilot.

project_description: A common approach to manage projects using JIRA
title: 100% JIRA Engagement for Researchers
details: Achieve full adoption of the new JIRA process among UX researchers. Monitor ticket creation rate and completeness; run follow-up check-ins; and resolve remaining blockers.

project_description: An AI-powered tool that reviews design output and scores alignment with the Design System standards
title: Assess the current adoption and usage of the Design System across the Design team.
details: Establish quantitative and qualitative baseline metrics for Design System adoption. Define KPIs (component coverage; token usage; contribution rate). This baseline is the reference point for the 50% and 100% targets.

project_description: Activities related to sharing work across the team.
goal: Strengthen cross-team collaboration and keep designers aligned across sites and time zones.
title: Improve Sprint DEMO (Templates)
details: Create a standard template for Sprint DEMO presentation

project_description: An AI-powered tool that reviews design output and scores alignment with the Design System standards
title: Complete Design System Guideline
details: Finalise and publish the comprehensive Design System usage guideline. Cover component selection; contribution workflow; naming conventions; and accessibility standards. Prerequisite for both the governance committee and the AI assistant rollout.

project_description: Practical and theoretical sessions on AI. Hosted by Erick.
title: Using Claude skills to build out designs in Lyra
details: Live demo of Claude custom skills applied to Lyra design production. Show how to accelerate component creation; generate design variants; and document patterns directly from the design tool.

project_description: A structured program to increase design quality and team efficiency through practical AI adoption
title: Assess the current AI usage across the Design team.
details: Understand the current approach and how much work is currently delegated to AI.

project_description: A common approach to manage projects using JIRA
title: 100% JIRA Engagement for Designers
details: Extend JIRA adoption to the full design team. Run onboarding for designers; adapt ticket templates where needed; and validate that all active design work is tracked. Completes team-wide JIRA coverage; creates the data foundation for the AI Assistant.

project_description: Human Committee for Governance: Q&A and critical decisions around the Design System
goal: x2 Governance in NICE apps; using human support
title: Introducing a process for the (Human) Committee for Design System Governance
details: Launch the cross-functional Design System governance committee. Define membership; meeting cadence; decision rights; and escalation paths. The committee will oversee both human and AI-driven governance going forward; directly precedes the AI assistant production rollout.

project_description: Practical and theoretical sessions on AI. Hosted by Erick.
title: Using Obsidian to manage .md files
details: Session on knowledge management for the design team. Set up a shared Obsidian vault for design decisions; research notes; and process docs. Show how Claude can query and summarize the vault. Follows the tool-heavy sessions; shifts focus to long-term knowledge capture.

details: Session on running local AI models (Ollama; LM Studio) for privacy-sensitive design work. Cover use cases where cloud AI is not appropriate and how local models complement the team's Claude setup. Prepares the team for independent AI experimentation beyond the sprint cycle.

project_description: Practical and theoretical sessions on AI. Hosted by Erick.
title: User Insights project overview
details: Presentation of the User Insights initiative and its connection to AI workflows. Show how ongoing research feeds the AI assistant and how automated insight synthesis can accelerate product decisions. Penultimate session; sets context for the final Storybook session.

project_description: An AI assistant that streamlines JIRA usage for designers and managers; reducing manual effort and improving tracking accuracy

title: Building a storybook with Claude
details: Final training session of the year: using Claude to generate; maintain; and document Storybook components. Bridge the gap between design system governance and engineering handoff. Closes the training program; outputs feed directly into Governance pillar.

title: Present the Playbook

title: 100% Designers Engagement for the Audit AI Assistant

project_description: An AI assistant that streamlines JIRA usage for designers and managers; reducing manual effort and improving tracking accuracy
title: Reduce time spent in JIRA by 50% through AI Assistant usage.
details: Scale AI Assistant optimisations to reach the 50% efficiency target. Automate recurring JIRA rituals (sprint review prep; backlog grooming summaries); and measure against the S196 baseline. End-of-year efficiency milestone; validates the full Efficiency pillar ROI.
